coverage.test: TestPanelGate_FailOpen_ActuallyCompletes — no review/ tree at all; asserts err==nil AND ex.completeCalled==true AND res.BeadClosed==true. Not 'no error': the terminal CompleteBead must observably run, so a panel-less complete that short-circuited to a no-op would fail.

TestPanelGate_SubThreshold_Blocks — registered fresh sub-threshold (2/6 APPROVE) panel for THIS bead; asserts err!=nil, ex.completeCalled==false (block is PRE-merge, before CommitAll/CompleteBead), res==nil, msg passes guard.HasFinalRecoveryLine, contains the raw `git merge bead/<id>` fence, and names the APPROVE tally. RED-on-revert to advisory-only.

TestPanelGate_Freshness_PassAndStaleBlock — fresh subtest pins reviewed_head_sha==rev-parse(bead/<id>) (precondition asserted) → pass + completeCalled; stale subtest uses deadbeef SHA with threshold MET so only staleness can block → err with 'commits landed after review' AND completeCalled==false.
TestPanelGate_Hatch_SkipEnv — panelSkipEnvFn forced true; sub-threshold panel does NOT block, completeCalled==true.

TestPanelGate_Hatch_ConfigToggle — panelGate(...,enabled=false) over a sub-threshold panel returns no error and still returns the matched reg (for audit); sanity arm with enabled=true on the SAME panel blocks, proving the toggle (not a mis-wired fixture) suppressed the block.
TestPanelGate_BlockNeverNamesSkipVar (gate-level) + TestPanelGateDecision table HC-7 assertion across every Block row (decision-level) — block message never contains panel.SkipPanelEnv.
TestPanelGate_DifferentBead_DoesNotBlock — sub-threshold panel registered for bead X (BeadID + slug bdX); completing bead Y proceeds with completeCalled==true, proving panel.ForBead isolation.

TestPanelGate_SharedDecisionPin — reconstructs facts exactly as panelGate does (panel.ForBead → PanelDirScanRoot → ResolveGateFacts with same GateIO seams → PanelGateDecision) and asserts the gate's err string is HasPrefix(want.Message). Confirmed non-vacuous: guard.FormatFailure puts the body FIRST then appends 'recovery:' lines, so the decision body IS a true prefix — pins that the in-binary path neither rewords nor re-derives the decision the hook produces.

Ordering verified in complete.go: panelGate is step 2.25, BEFORE step-2.5 exec.CommitAll, bd close, and step-4 exec.CompleteBead. The block tests assert completeCalled==false (and res==nil), pinning that nothing irreversible ran. The fresh-pass test asserts reviewed_head_sha==pre-CommitAll bead tip, catching a future move of the gate after CommitAll (which would advance the tip and false-fire staleness).

vacuous_or_missing: None blocking. The fixtures stand up a REAL temp git repo (rev-parse runs against a real bead/<id> ref) and write REAL panel.json + verdict files to disk (writePanel); the ONLY stub is CompleteBead (the irreversible merge), surfaced via the observable completeCalled flag. So a no-op gate could NOT pass these: fail-open/skip/different-bead REQUIRE completeCalled==true and block tests REQUIRE completeCalled==false — the gate's effect is directly observed, not asserted against a loose mock.

Minor (non-blocking) note: the abandoned-panel Warn path (decision clause 3) and the transient-gitErr/missing-ref Warn paths are pinned at the decision level (TestPanelGateDecision table + precomplete_run_test.go RealGit tests) but not re-exercised through complete.Run's e2e harness. Acceptable: the SharedDecisionPin test proves complete.Run delegates verbatim to the same pure decision over the same ResolveGateFacts wiring, so the Warn-path behavior is transitively covered without a redundant e2e per clause.
summary: APPROVE. All 6 mandated behaviors are genuinely pinned, plus gate-ordering and different-bead isolation. `go test -count=1 -run 'TestPanel|TestGate' ./internal/complete/ ./internal/panel/` PASSES (all 8 TestPanelGate_* e2e subtests + the full TestPanelGateDecision matrix); precomplete_run_test.go also PASSES. All three test files are hermetic and CI-runnable (std + internal/{gitutil,config,panel,workspace,executor,guard} only — NO internal/harness import, so they don't risk the multi-minute hang). The fail-open and block assertions are non-vacuous because they observe the real terminal mutation (completeCalled) and a non-nil/nil result, not just err==nil — a no-op gate would fail them. SharedDecisionPin's HasPrefix is load-bearing: guard.FormatFailure emits body-then-recovery, so it truly pins verbatim delegation. Freshness uses threshold-MET panels so staleness is the sole isolated variable; sub-threshold uses fresh SHA so threshold is isolated — clean single-variable pins. HC-7 is double-covered (gate + decision table). No vacuous assertions, no missing spec-critical case.
